# Accessibility Reference

Use this reference when building forms, dialogs, navigation, tables, or interactive UI.
Accessibility is not a feature — it is a baseline quality requirement.

---

## Core Principles

1. **Perceivable** — Content must be available to all senses (sight, hearing, touch).
2. **Operable** — All interactive elements must be usable with keyboard, mouse, and assistive tech.
3. **Understandable** — UI must be predictable, readable, and provide clear feedback.
4. **Robust** — Markup must be valid and compatible with assistive technologies.

---

## Keyboard Interaction

Every interactive element must be operable with a keyboard.

### Focus Management

```tsx
// React
<div
  role="button"
  tabIndex={0}
  onClick={handleAction}
  onKeyDown={(e) => {
    if (e.key === "Enter" || e.key === " ") {
      e.preventDefault();
      handleAction();
    }
  }}
>
  Custom Action
</div>
```

```vue
<!-- Vue -->
<div
  role="button"
  tabindex="0"
  @click="handleAction"
  @keydown.enter.space.prevent="handleAction"
>
  Custom Action
</div>

<!-- Better in both: use a real button -->
<button @click="handleAction">Custom Action</button>
```

### Focus Visibility

Always maintain visible focus indicators. Never remove outlines without providing an alternative.

```css
/* Never do this */
*:focus {
  outline: none;
}

/* Do this — visible focus for keyboard users */
:focus-visible {
  outline: 2px solid var(--color-focus);
  outline-offset: 2px;
}
```

### Expected Keyboard Patterns

| Component | Keys |
|---|---|
| Button | `Enter`, `Space` to activate |
| Link | `Enter` to follow |
| Checkbox | `Space` to toggle |
| Radio group | `Arrow` keys to move, `Space` to select |
| Tabs | `Arrow` keys to move, `Enter`/`Space` to activate (or auto-activate) |
| Dialog | `Escape` to close, focus trapped inside |
| Menu | `Arrow` keys to navigate, `Enter` to select, `Escape` to close |
| Combobox | `Arrow` keys to navigate options, `Enter` to select, `Escape` to close |

---

## Accessible Names and Labels

Every interactive element must have an accessible name.

### Forms

```html
<!-- Visible label (preferred) -->
<label for="email">Email Address</label>
<input id="email" type="email" />

<!-- Wrapping label -->
<label>
  Email Address
  <input type="email" />
</label>

<!-- Icon-only button needs aria-label -->
<button aria-label="Close dialog">
  <CloseIcon aria-hidden="true" />
</button>

<!-- Group label with fieldset -->
<fieldset>
  <legend>Shipping Address</legend>
  <label for="street">Street</label>
  <input id="street" name="street" />
</fieldset>
```

### Images

```html
<!-- Informative image -->
<img src="chart.png" alt="Revenue increased 25% in Q4 2025" />

<!-- Decorative image -->
<img src="divider.svg" alt="" />

<!-- Complex image with extended description -->
<figure>
  <img src="architecture.png" alt="System architecture diagram" />
  <figcaption>
    The system consists of three layers: API gateway, service mesh, and data store.
  </figcaption>
</figure>

<!-- Icon next to text (decorative) -->
<button>
  <SearchIcon aria-hidden="true" />
  Search
</button>
```

---

## ARIA Patterns

### Dialog / Modal

```tsx
// React
function Dialog({ isOpen, onClose, title, children }: DialogProps) {
  const dialogRef = React.useRef<HTMLDialogElement>(null);

  React.useEffect(() => {
    const dialog = dialogRef.current;
    if (!dialog) return;
    if (isOpen) { dialog.showModal(); } else { dialog.close(); }
  }, [isOpen]);

  return (
    <dialog ref={dialogRef} onClose={onClose} aria-labelledby="dialog-title">
      <header>
        <h2 id="dialog-title">{title}</h2>
        <button onClick={onClose} aria-label="Close">
          <CloseIcon aria-hidden="true" />
        </button>
      </header>
      <div>{children}</div>
    </dialog>
  );
}
```

```vue
<!-- Vue -->
<script setup lang="ts">
import { ref, watch } from "vue";

const props = defineProps<{ open: boolean; title: string }>();
const emit = defineEmits<{ close: [] }>();
const dialogRef = ref<HTMLDialogElement>();

watch(() => props.open, (isOpen) => {
  if (isOpen) { dialogRef.value?.showModal(); } else { dialogRef.value?.close(); }
});
</script>

<template>
  <dialog ref="dialogRef" @close="emit('close')" aria-labelledby="dialog-title">
    <header>
      <h2 id="dialog-title">{{ title }}</h2>
      <button @click="emit('close')" aria-label="Close">
        <CloseIcon aria-hidden="true" />
      </button>
    </header>
    <div><slot /></div>
  </dialog>
</template>
```
```

Rules:
- Use native `<dialog>` element when possible.
- Focus must be trapped inside the dialog while open.
- `Escape` must close the dialog.
- Focus returns to the trigger element on close.
- Background content must be inert (`<dialog>` handles this natively with `showModal()`).

### Tabs

```tsx
<div role="tablist" aria-label="Account settings">
  <button
    role="tab"
    id="tab-profile"
    aria-selected={activeTab === "profile"}
    aria-controls="panel-profile"
    tabIndex={activeTab === "profile" ? 0 : -1}
    onClick={() => setActiveTab("profile")}
  >
    Profile
  </button>
  <button
    role="tab"
    id="tab-security"
    aria-selected={activeTab === "security"}
    aria-controls="panel-security"
    tabIndex={activeTab === "security" ? 0 : -1}
    onClick={() => setActiveTab("security")}
  >
    Security
  </button>
</div>

<div
  role="tabpanel"
  id="panel-profile"
  aria-labelledby="tab-profile"
  hidden={activeTab !== "profile"}
>
  Profile content
</div>
```

Rules:
- Arrow keys navigate between tabs.
- Only the active tab is in the tab order (`tabIndex={0}`).
- Each tab has `aria-controls` pointing to its panel.
- Each panel has `aria-labelledby` pointing to its tab.

### Combobox / Autocomplete

```tsx
<div>
  <label htmlFor="city-input">City</label>
  <input
    id="city-input"
    role="combobox"
    aria-expanded={isOpen}
    aria-controls="city-listbox"
    aria-activedescendant={activeOptionId}
    aria-autocomplete="list"
    value={query}
    onChange={(e) => setQuery(e.target.value)}
  />
  {isOpen && (
    <ul id="city-listbox" role="listbox">
      {options.map((option) => (
        <li
          key={option.id}
          id={`city-option-${option.id}`}
          role="option"
          aria-selected={option.id === selectedId}
          onClick={() => selectOption(option)}
        >
          {option.label}
        </li>
      ))}
    </ul>
  )}
</div>
```

### Toast / Notification

```tsx
// React
<div role="status" aria-live="polite">
  {message && <p>{message}</p>}
</div>

<div role="alert" aria-live="assertive">
  {error && <p>{error}</p>}
</div>
```

```vue
<!-- Vue -->
<div role="status" aria-live="polite">
  <p v-if="message">{{ message }}</p>
</div>

<div role="alert" aria-live="assertive">
  <p v-if="error">{{ error }}</p>
</div>
```

Rules:
- Use `role="status"` with `aria-live="polite"` for non-urgent updates.
- Use `role="alert"` with `aria-live="assertive"` for errors and urgent messages.
- The live region element must exist in the DOM before content is injected.

---

## Error and Validation Messaging

```tsx
// React
<div>
  <label htmlFor="password">Password</label>
  <input
    id="password"
    type="password"
    aria-describedby="password-hint password-error"
    aria-invalid={!!error}
    required
  />
  <p id="password-hint" className="hint">Must be at least 8 characters</p>
  {error && (
    <p id="password-error" role="alert" className="error">{error}</p>
  )}
</div>
```

```vue
<!-- Vue -->
<div>
  <label for="password">Password</label>
  <input
    id="password"
    type="password"
    aria-describedby="password-hint password-error"
    :aria-invalid="!!error"
    required
  />
  <p id="password-hint" class="hint">Must be at least 8 characters</p>
  <p v-if="error" id="password-error" role="alert" class="error">
    {{ error }}
  </p>
</div>
```

Rules:
- Use `aria-describedby` to associate hints and errors with inputs.
- Use `aria-invalid="true"` when the field has a validation error.
- Error messages should use `role="alert"` to be announced immediately.
- Keep error text next to the associated field, not in a remote summary only.

---

## Loading and Disabled States

```tsx
// React
<button disabled={isLoading} aria-busy={isLoading}>
  {isLoading ? "Saving…" : "Save"}
</button>

<section aria-busy={isLoading} aria-label="User list">
  {isLoading ? <SkeletonList /> : <UserList users={users} />}
</section>
```

```vue
<!-- Vue -->
<button :disabled="isLoading" :aria-busy="isLoading">
  {{ isLoading ? "Saving…" : "Save" }}
</button>

<section :aria-busy="isLoading" aria-label="User list">
  <SkeletonList v-if="isLoading" />
  <UserList v-else :users="users" />
</section>

<!-- Disabled with explanation -->
<div>
  <button disabled aria-describedby="upgrade-hint">Export to PDF</button>
  <p id="upgrade-hint" class="hint">Available on Pro plan</p>
</div>
```

---

## Skip Navigation

Provide a skip link for keyboard users to bypass repetitive navigation.

```html
<body>
  <a href="#main-content" className="skip-link">
    Skip to main content
  </a>
  <nav>...</nav>
  <main id="main-content">...</main>
</body>
```

```css
.skip-link {
  position: absolute;
  top: -100%;
  left: 0;
  z-index: 100;
  padding: 0.5rem 1rem;
}

.skip-link:focus {
  top: 0;
}
```

---

## Color and Contrast

- Text must meet WCAG AA contrast ratio: 4.5:1 for normal text, 3:1 for large text.
- Never use color alone to convey information. Pair with text, icons, or patterns.
- Focus indicators must have 3:1 contrast against adjacent colors.

```html
<!-- Wrong: color alone -->
<span style="color: red">Error</span>

<!-- Correct: color + icon + text -->
<span className="error">
  <AlertIcon aria-hidden="true" /> Error: Email is required
</span>
```

---

## Motion and Animation

- Respect `prefers-reduced-motion`.
- Never rely on animation to convey critical information.

```css
@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) {
  *,
  *::before,
  *::after {
    animation-duration: 0.01ms !important;
    animation-iteration-count: 1 !important;
    transition-duration: 0.01ms !important;
  }
}
```

---

## Touch Targets

Ensure interactive elements are large enough for touch input.

- Minimum touch target size: 44x44 CSS pixels (WCAG 2.5.8).
- If the visible element is smaller (e.g., icon button), expand the clickable area with padding or pseudo-elements.

```css
/* Small icon button with expanded touch target */
.icon-button {
  position: relative;
  width: 24px;
  height: 24px;
}

.icon-button::after {
  content: "";
  position: absolute;
  inset: -10px; /* Expands to 44x44 */
}
```

---

## Focus Management in SPAs

Single-page apps break the browser's native focus behavior on navigation. You must manage focus manually.

### After Route Change

```tsx
// React (Next.js)
"use client";

import { usePathname } from "next/navigation";
import { useEffect, useRef } from "react";

function RouteAnnouncer() {
  const pathname = usePathname();
  const ref = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null);

  useEffect(() => {
    ref.current?.focus();
  }, [pathname]);

  return (
    <div ref={ref} tabIndex={-1} role="status" aria-live="polite" className="sr-only">
      Navigated to {pathname}
    </div>
  );
}
```

```vue
<!-- Vue (Nuxt) — using a plugin or layout component -->
<script setup lang="ts">
import { ref, watch } from "vue";

const route = useRoute();
const announcer = ref<HTMLDivElement>();

watch(() => route.path, () => {
  nextTick(() => announcer.value?.focus());
});
</script>

<template>
  <div ref="announcer" tabindex="-1" role="status" aria-live="polite" class="sr-only">
    Navigated to {{ route.path }}
  </div>
</template>
```

### After Dynamic Content Load

```tsx
// React
useEffect(() => {
  if (results.length > 0) {
    firstResultRef.current?.focus();
  }
}, [results]);
```

```vue
<!-- Vue -->
<script setup lang="ts">
const firstResult = ref<HTMLElement>();

watch(results, (newResults) => {
  if (newResults.length > 0) {
    nextTick(() => firstResult.value?.focus());
  }
});
</script>
```

### After Item Deletion

```tsx
// React
function handleDelete(index: number) {
  deleteItem(index);
  const nextFocusTarget =
    itemRefs.current[index + 1] ?? itemRefs.current[index - 1] ?? listRef.current;
  nextFocusTarget?.focus();
}
```

```vue
<!-- Vue -->
<script setup lang="ts">
const itemRefs = ref<HTMLElement[]>([]);
const listRef = ref<HTMLElement>();

function handleDelete(index: number) {
  deleteItem(index);
  nextTick(() => {
    const next = itemRefs.value[index] ?? itemRefs.value[index - 1] ?? listRef.value;
    next?.focus();
  });
}
</script>
```

---

## Disclosure Pattern

```tsx
// Using native <details>/<summary> (preferred when possible)
<details>
  <summary>Filter options</summary>
  <fieldset>
    <legend>Status</legend>
    {/* filter controls */}
  </fieldset>
</details>

// Custom disclosure with ARIA
<div>
  <button
    aria-expanded={isOpen}
    aria-controls="filter-panel"
    onClick={() => setIsOpen(!isOpen)}
  >
    Filter options
  </button>
  <div id="filter-panel" hidden={!isOpen}>
    {/* filter controls */}
  </div>
</div>
```

---

## Sortable Table

```tsx
<table>
  <caption>Team members</caption>
  <thead>
    <tr>
      <th scope="col" aria-sort={sortField === "name" ? sortDirection : "none"}>
        <button onClick={() => toggleSort("name")}>
          Name
          <SortIcon direction={sortField === "name" ? sortDirection : undefined} />
        </button>
      </th>
      <th scope="col" aria-sort={sortField === "role" ? sortDirection : "none"}>
        <button onClick={() => toggleSort("role")}>
          Role
          <SortIcon direction={sortField === "role" ? sortDirection : undefined} />
        </button>
      </th>
    </tr>
  </thead>
  <tbody>
    {/* rows */}
  </tbody>
</table>

{/* Announce sort changes */}
<div role="status" aria-live="polite" className="sr-only">
  {sortField && `Sorted by ${sortField}, ${sortDirection}`}
</div>
```

Rules:
- Use `aria-sort` on `<th>`: `"ascending"`, `"descending"`, or `"none"`.
- The sort control should be a `<button>` inside the `<th>`.
- Announce sort changes via a live region.

---

## Responsive Accessibility

Accessibility requirements do not change on small screens, but implementation details may.

- Touch targets must meet 44x44px minimum.
- Mobile screen readers (VoiceOver, TalkBack) navigate differently — ensure swipe gestures don't conflict.
- `hover`-only interactions (tooltips) must have non-hover alternatives on touch devices.
- Off-screen elements (mobile nav, drawers) must use `inert` or `hidden` when closed.
- Zoom to 200% must not break layout or hide content.

```css
/* Ensure text remains readable at 200% zoom */
body {
  max-width: 100vw;
  overflow-x: hidden;
}
```

---

## Testing Checklist

- [ ] Can every interactive element be reached and activated with keyboard only?
- [ ] Does every interactive element have a visible focus indicator?
- [ ] Does every form input have a visible, associated label?
- [ ] Are error messages associated with their inputs via `aria-describedby`?
- [ ] Do dynamic content changes use live regions?
- [ ] Can the page be understood without color?
- [ ] Does the dialog trap focus and return it on close?
- [ ] Are decorative images marked with `alt=""`?
- [ ] Does the heading hierarchy make logical sense?
- [ ] Are touch targets at least 44x44px?
- [ ] Is focus managed correctly after route changes, deletions, and dynamic content loads?
- [ ] Are off-screen elements (drawers, mobile nav) inert when closed?
- [ ] Does the layout remain usable at 200% zoom?
